title: Amphetamine: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
lorpheniramine or cetirizine). Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking amphetamine. Do not start any of these medications while taking amphetamine without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you have hyperthyroidism (condition in which there is too much thyroid hormone in the body), or strong feelings of anxiety, tension, or agitation. Your doctor will probably tell you not to take amphetamine. tell your doctor if anyone in your family has or has ever had an irregular heartbeat or has died suddenly. 
Also tell your doctor if you have recently had a heart attack or if you have or have ever had a heart defect, arteriosclerosis (hardening of the arteries), coronary artery disease (narrowing of the blood vessels that supply blood to the heart), high blood pressure, an irregular heartbeat, cardiomyopathy (thickening of the heart muscles), 
heart or blood vessel disease, or other heart problems. 
Your doctor will examine you to see if your heart and blood vessels are healthy. 
Your doctor will probably tell you not to take amphetamine if you have a heart condition or if there is a high risk that you may develop a heart condition. tell your doctor if you or anyone in your family has or has ever had depression, bipolar disorder (mood that changes from depressed to abnormally excited), mania (
